title: Auto-advance questions and display in continuous vertical layout

requirements.text: Remove the Next button from the test interface so students no longer need to press it to move to the next question

quotes: To.move on to next Question no next button needed to press

acceptanceCriteria: Next button is not visible during test-taking
Students can still navigate through questions without clicking Next

requirements.text: Display all test questions in a continuous vertical row, one after another, instead of showing one question at a time

quotes: Keep them in a row one after another

acceptanceCriteria: All questions appear vertically stacked on the same page
Students can scroll through all questions without pagination
Answer inputs for all questions are visible simultaneously

requirements.text: Maintain the reading test two-panel layout with passage on the left and all questions displayed vertically on the right panel
acceptanceCriteria: Reading tests continue to show passage in left panel
All reading test questions appear in continuous vertical scroll in right panel

requirements.text: Update progress tracking and navigation to work with the continuous question display instead of current question index
acceptanceCriteria: Progress indicator reflects which questions have been answered
Submit button remains accessible after all questions
Timer and fullscreen controls continue to function correctly

constraints: Preserve the existing two-panel ReadingTestLayout for reading tests
Maintain all existing question types and media display functionality
Keep the timer, fullscreen mode, and submit functionality intact
Do not modify backend test structure or response handling
nonGoals: Changing the test creation or management interfaces
Modifying authentication or student registration flows
Altering the results display or scoring logic
Adding new question types or test formats
